name: Calzones
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup Whole wheat flour or 1 c. spelt flour
2 cups Crumbled firm tofu (1 12-oz. package, drained)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water at about 105 degrees or just warm to touch. Let sit for about 5 minutes until the yeast starts coming to the top of the water.
2. Add oil, salt, and Sucanat to the yeast mixture and mix well.
3. Gradually add whole wheat flour and mix with a wooden spoon, scraping the sides of the bowl often. Stir for about 150 strokes as the gluten strands will be forming.
4. Continue adding the remaining flour until the dough is smooth and no longer sticky.
5. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead for about 5 minutes without adding extra flour to avoid making the final product too dry.
6. Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for about an hour.
7. Meanwhile, prepare the filling by heating oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add onions and cook until transparent. Stir occasionally to prevent burning.
8. Add mushrooms, celery, tomatoes, olives, basil, oregano, tomato paste, nutritional yeast, arrowroot, and salsa (if desired). Cook for about 5 minutes or until the vegetables are softened.
9. Mix in crumbled tofu and season with salt and pepper.
10. When the dough has risen to double its size, punch it down and divide into six equal portions.
11. Roll each portion into a circle that is about 1/4 inch thick. Place about 1/6 of the filling on half of each circle, leaving a 1/2-inch rim around the edges.
12. Fold the dough over to enclose the filling, stretching it if necessary. Press down around the outside with a fork dipped in water to prevent sticking.
13. Place calzones on an oiled baking sheet and brush lightly with oil on top.
14. Make a few incisions on each calzone with a knife.
15. Bake in a preheated oven at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for 15-18 minutes or until golden brown. If your oven is small, you may need to bake in batches.
16. Serve hot and enjoy!
